title: About
text:
ABOUT
  
  An open-source, principles-first curriculum for understanding how machine learning systems are built, optimized, and deployed at every scale.

title: About
text:
Mission

The world is rushing to build AI systems. It is not engineering them.
Machine learning has transformed every industry, but the education pipeline has not kept pace. Students learn algorithms in isolation, divorced from the silicon that executes them, the memory hierarchies that constrain them, and the networks that distribute them. The result: a generation of practitioners who can call model.fit() but cannot reason about why their model is slow, where the bottleneck lives, or how to fix it without guessing.
Machine Learning Systems exists to close that gap. We teach AI as infrastructure — governed by physical laws, shaped by engineering constraints, and measurable through quantitative reasoning. Our thesis is simple: constraints drive architecture. You do not choose a Transformer because it is trendy; you choose it because of how it parallelizes on real silicon.

  
    ⚙️
    Principles Over APIs
    We teach the physics beneath the framework. Memory hierarchies, not torch.cuda(). Arithmetic intensity, not library calls. Knowledge that endures beyond any single tool.
  
  
    📐
    Quantitative Reasoning
    Every claim is backed by a number. Students learn to estimate, measure, and verify — the same skills that separate a systems engineer from a script runner.
  
  
    🌍
    Open and Global
    Free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. Used at universities across six continents. Licensed under CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 so anyone can teach from it, adapt it, and build on it.
  
  
    🔬
    Full-Stack Curriculum
    Not just a textbook. TinyTorch, hardware kits, interactive labs, lecture slides, and instructor resources — everything needed to teach a two-semester course.
  



  
    22,800+
    GitHub Stars

    Volumes

title: About
text:
Team

Machine Learning Systems is a collaborative effort led by Harvard University and shaped by contributors spanning academia, industry, and the global TinyML community.

  
    
    Vijay Janapa Reddi
    Lead Author
    Associate Professor, Harvard University
  
  
    
    Marcelo Rovai
    Hardware Kits Lead
    Professor, UNIFEI
  
  
    
    Zeljko Herold
    Core Contributor
    Community
  
  
    
    Jason Jabbour
    Core Contributor
    Harvard University
  
  
    
    Colby Banbury
    MLPerf / Benchmarks
    Harvard University
  


Academic Network
The curriculum builds on the broader TinyMLedu Open Education Initiative, a global partnership with the tinyML Foundation, ICTP, Google, Arduino, and Edge Impulse. The TinyML4D Academic Network connects 40+ universities across six continents, sharing course materials and conducting collaborative research.

text:
Contributors

This curriculum is built by an open-source community. Every chapter, diagram, lab, and code review reflects the expertise of contributors from around the world.

title: About
text:
Adopters

Machine Learning Systems is taught at universities and used by organizations worldwide. If your institution uses this curriculum, let us know — we would love to feature you here.

  
    Harvard University
    CS 249r · Cambridge, MA
  
  
    Barnard College, Columbia
    Embedded ML · New York, NY
  
  
    UNIFEI
    TinyML · Itajubá, Brazil
  
  
    ICTP
    SciTinyML · Trieste, Italy
  



  Teaching ML Systems?
  Our Instructor Hub provides everything you need: syllabi, assessment rubrics, lecture slides, and TA resources for a two-semester curriculum.

title: About
text:
Press

Selected milestones and coverage for the Machine Learning Systems curriculum.

  
    
    
      MIT Press Publication
      Two-volume hardcover edition forthcoming from MIT Press, Summer 2026.
      2026
    
  
  
    
    
      22,800+ GitHub Stars
      Among the most-starred open-source textbook repositories in machine learning and systems engineering.
      March 2026
    
  
  
    
    
      IEEE Citation
      Formally citable as an IEEE publication for academic research and coursework.
      2024
    
  
  
    
    
      Global Reach via TinyMLedu
      Curriculum materials used across 40+ universities and six continents through the TinyML4D Academic Network and ICTP partnership.
      2021–present
    
  
  
    
    
      edX Professional Certificate
      Foundational content contributed to the HarvardX TinyML Professional Certificate on edX, completed by thousands of learners worldwide.
      2020–present

title: About

License

Machine Learning Systems is released under the Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0).
This means you are free to share and adapt the material for non-commercial purposes, as long as you give appropriate credit and distribute your contributions under the same license.
